_meta.title: base_station_indicator_library
_meta.description: Library of customer-facing text for every indicator at every band of the base_station chain. Layered annotation ON TOP OF the canonical chain definition (base_station_confidence_score_v2_1_LOCKED.json). References chain indicator IDs (L3I_BASE_NNN). When chain weights or threshold ranges change, library entries for affected indicators need review for consistency.

three_tier_architecture.tier_1_chain_definition: base_station_confidence_score_v2_1_LOCKED.json — canonical scoring, chain logic, indicator weights, threshold values
three_tier_architecture.tier_2_library_this_file: base_station_indicator_library_v2_1.json — customer-voice text per indicator per band, derivation rationale

_meta.text_authorship_status: CLAUDE-DRAFTED PLACEHOLDER. Per user Q1 confirmation during v2.1 lock session: structure locked, library text marked as placeholder pending domain refinement.

schema_notes.verified_statement: one-sentence statement shown when this indicator is in its good band
schema_notes.is_critical_path: true if this indicator can fire a hard gate (forces overall score=0)
schema_notes.bands: array of band entries with score_range, level (good/review/resurvey/minor), label, impact, actions
schema_notes.derivation: threshold reasoning shown in auditor view
schema_notes.flag: flag raised when indicator scores out of good band (null if no flag wired)

L3I_BASE_001.name: Coverage
L3I_BASE_001.verified_statement: Base recorded through the entire flight window with adequate pre-flight and post-flight buffer.

bands.label: Full coverage + ≥2 min pre-flight + ≥60s post-flight buffer
Full coverage but pre-flight buffer <60s
bands.impact: PPK convergence may be incomplete at flight start. Early position estimates can carry residual error.
actions: Start base recording 2–3 min before takeoff next time
Review processed sigma values on early epochs
Trim any noisy early data if quality is critical

bands.label: Base not recording during part of flight (HARD GATE)
bands.impact: PPK cannot correct flight epochs without base coverage. Affected portions have only autonomous-fix accuracy.
actions: Recollect base with adequate flight-window coverage
Start base recording 2–3 min before takeoff
Stop base only after motors off (60s post-landing)

L3I_BASE_001.derivation: Score 100 (band [89-100]) reflects industry PPK convergence guidance (major receiver manufacturers recommend 2-min pre-flight buffer). Score 88 (band [73-88]) reflects pre-flight buffer 60-120s, convergence possible but compromised. Score 72 (band [40-72]) reflects pre<60s, drives review. Hard gate at 0 when coverage gap means PPK has no reference for affected epochs. (v2.1.1 boundary patch: bands shifted from [88-100]/[72-87]/[40-71] to [89-100]/[73-88]/[40-72] so pipeline-emitted scores 100/88/72 land in the intended bands 1/2/3.)

L3I_BASE_002.name: Integrity
L3I_BASE_002.verified_statement: Base session ran end-to-end without interruption, log uploaded.

bands.label: Clean session, no interruptions, log uploaded
Operation Log absent — session integrity unconfirmed
bands.impact: Without the operation log, we can't confirm the base shut down cleanly. RINEX may still be usable but the audit trail is incomplete.
actions: Verify RINEX file size matches expected duration
Inspect last epoch timestamp against planned session end
Ensure operation log uploads with base files next time

bands.label: Session interrupted (unexpected shutdown)
bands.impact: Base shut down unexpectedly during recording. RINEX may be truncated or corrupted near the shutdown event.
actions: Inspect RINEX around the shutdown timestamp
If shutdown was outside flight window, data may still process
If shutdown was during flight, recollect

L3I_BASE_002.derivation: Score 60 (operation log absent) is conservative middle band — data may be fine but cannot verify shutdown cleanliness. Score 20 (interrupted) reflects flight-window shutdowns produce unrecoverable gaps. (Q3 lock: stays critical for v2.1; v2 enhancement to check timestamp overlap with flight window.)

L3I_BASE_003.name: Format
L3I_BASE_003.verified_statement: RINEX is in a supported version with complete header and dual-frequency observations.

bands.label: Single-frequency or incomplete header
bands.impact: Single-frequency means ionospheric error cannot be modeled — accuracy degrades during active solar weather. Header gaps may need manual patching before processing.
actions: Verify receiver was configured for dual-frequency
Patch missing header fields (antenna, marker) if known
Expect reduced accuracy under high Kp index

bands.label: RINEX version not supported by PPK software
bands.impact: Processor cannot ingest this file directly. Data itself is fine — format conversion required, then resubmit.
actions: Convert RINEX to a supported version (3.x or 2.11)
Use vendor converter or standard tool (e.g., teqc, gfzrnx)
Verify converted file passes format validation, then resubmit

L3I_BASE_003.derivation: Q2 lock applied: version-unsupported downgraded from resurvey to review. Data is fine; format is fixable via conversion. The operational action is convert+resubmit, not recollect.

L3I_BASE_004.name: Continuity
L3I_BASE_004.verified_statement: Continuous observations through the session with minimal cycle slips.

bands.label: No gaps or minor only (<60s), minimal cycle slips
Gap >60s detected — PPK must re-converge
bands.impact: Base lost satellite tracking for >60s. PPK must re-converge after the gap, reducing accuracy in that window.
actions: Check whether photos were taken during the gap
Investigate cause (signal blockage, brief power loss)
Position base in clearer location next time

L3I_BASE_004.derivation: 60s gap threshold from industry PPK best practice. Cycle slip count weighted by satellite — count below 5% of total epochs is normal.

L3I_BASE_005.name: Antenna height
L3I_BASE_005.verified_statement: Antenna height documented to ARP, with field measurement matching RINEX header.

bands.label: Slant measurement or conflicts with RINEX delta-H
bands.impact: Antenna height measurement carries higher uncertainty. Wrong height shifts all output elevations systematically.
actions: Verify slant-to-vertical conversion was applied
Reconcile form value against RINEX delta-H
Re-measure if uncertain — measure vertical to ARP

bands.label: Antenna height not entered (HARD GATE)
bands.impact: PPK output elevations have unknown reference. Without antenna height, output is meaningless regardless of how clean everything else is.
actions: Enter antenna height now if known from field notes
If not recoverable, redo base setup with measured height
Measure 3 times to ARP, average for best confidence

L3I_BASE_005.derivation: Q1 lock applied: antenna height is a true hard gate (forces overall score to 0). Without antenna height, PPK output elevations are uninterpretable regardless of how clean other blocks are. Q6 lock applied: global_gate_condition string updated to include this indicator.

L3I_BASE_006.name: Setup verification
L3I_BASE_006.verified_statement: Base set up over a known reference mark, ideally with second-person verification.

bands.label: Reused mark not re-verified this session
bands.impact: Mark may have shifted since last use. Coordinates carry historical uncertainty.
actions: Re-verify mark via static occupation + CORS
Compare PPK position against published coordinates
Document if verification not feasible

L3I_BASE_006.derivation: Score 50 captures unverified-mark concept — conceptually a third-state (provenance unverifiable) pending chain-library retrofit.

L3I_BASE_007.name: Antenna type match
L3I_BASE_007.verified_statement: Antenna model on form matches RINEX header.

bands.label: Form antenna differs from RINEX header
bands.impact: Wrong antenna profile means wrong ANTEX calibration — systematic position bias from millimeters to centimeters.
actions: Verify physical antenna against form selection
Update whichever source is incorrect (form or header)
Re-process with corrected ANTEX profile

L3I_BASE_007.derivation: Type-string consistency check only — not a true ANTEX phase-center calibration (Stage-2 concern in pre_processing chain).

L3I_BASE_008.name: Multipath
L3I_BASE_008.verified_statement: Clean signal environment — low C/N0 variance, no significant multipath risk.

bands.label: Low C/N0 variance — clean signal environment
Moderate C/N0 variance — partial multipath risk
bands.impact: Base was near reflective surfaces. PPK can usually handle it but residuals may show position oscillations.
actions: Check processed residuals for oscillation patterns
Position base ≥10m from buildings/vehicles/water next time
Choose open-sky locations for high-stakes work

bands.label: High C/N0 variance — significant multipath risk
bands.impact: Base was clearly in a reflective environment. PPK output likely has position oscillations or elevated residuals.
actions: Inspect PPK position output for wobble
Recollect at cleaner site if accuracy is critical
Scout base location for reflective surfaces in future

L3I_BASE_008.derivation: C/N0 variance as multipath proxy is a standard signal-quality metric. Threshold values are heuristic — calibration against measured PPK residuals is a future deliverable.

L3I_BASE_009.name: Ionospheric risk
L3I_BASE_009.verified_statement: Either calm geomagnetic conditions or dual-frequency observations (ionospheric error modeled).

bands.label: High Kp AND single-frequency only
bands.impact: Active solar weather + single-frequency means ionospheric delay cannot be modeled. Position errors can reach centimeters or more.
actions: Check NOAA SWPC Kp index for survey window
Prefer dual-frequency receiver for next survey
Reschedule if Kp ≥5 and accuracy is critical

L3I_BASE_009.derivation: Kp ≥5 threshold from NOAA space weather scale (G1+ geomagnetic storm). Quantitative impact depends on storm magnitude — qualitative flag only at v1.

L3I_BASE_010.name: PDOP
L3I_BASE_010.verified_statement: Strong satellite geometry — PDOP within standard ranges throughout session.

bands.label: PDOP >4 — marginal to poor geometry
bands.impact: Satellite geometry was suboptimal — PPK still works but position uncertainty is elevated.
actions: Check processed sigma values
Use mission planner for future surveys at this site
Reschedule if PDOP forecast >6 and accuracy is critical

L3I_BASE_010.derivation: Standard PDOP categorization from GNSS handbooks. 2/4/6 boundaries widely used across industry.

L3I_BASE_011.name: Acquisition
L3I_BASE_011.verified_statement: Base acquired satellites within normal startup time, no obstruction or interference signs.

bands.label: >3 min — slow startup (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Base was slow to lock onto satellites. Usually a cold start. Recurring slowness can indicate receiver health issues, but rarely affects this session's data quality.
actions: Check antenna location for obstructions
Verify firmware version and battery state
Service unit if slow acquisition is recurring

L3I_BASE_011.derivation: Q4 lock applied: downgraded from review to minor. Acquisition slowness is a hygiene signal, not a deliverable-quality concern. Q5 lock applied: minor findings hidden from default customer view; visible only in 'what passed / what else was checked' expandable as a subtle checklist row.
